prompt:
Our office lending library outgrew the spreadsheet, so: a CLI, python 3.12, stdlib only. I want at least `cli.py` (commands), `lending.py` (loan/fine math, pure functions, no file IO -- the kiosk imports this directly), and `store.py` (owns the JSON db format). Split out more modules if it helps you.

Every command takes `--db PATH` before the subcommand, every invocation is a separate process, so state lives in that JSON file. Dates are always explicit `YYYY-MM-DD` arguments -- never the wall clock -- because I replay sessions when someone disputes a fine. Malformed dates: nonzero exit. Sample session so you can see the shapes:

    $ python3 cli.py --db lib.json add-book 978-1 --title "Dune" --copies 2
    $ python3 cli.py --db lib.json checkout 978-1 --member ana --date 2026-04-01
    due 2026-04-15
    $ python3 cli.py --db lib.json return 978-1 --member ana --date 2026-04-20
    fine $1.25
    $ python3 cli.json --db lib.json status 978-1
    Dune	available=2/2	holds=0

(ignore the typo, you get it -- `status` prints exactly `TITLE<TAB>available=A/N<TAB>holds=K`.)

The rules, which are the whole point:
- Loans run 14 days: checkout on the 1st is due on the 15th. `lending.due_date('2026-04-01') -> '2026-04-15'`. Checkout prints exactly `due YYYY-MM-DD`.
- Fines are 25 cents per day late, computed at return time: `lending.late_fee_cents(due, returned) -> int` (0 for on-time or early). Return prints exactly `fine $X.XX` -- `fine $0.00` when on time. Do the money in integer cents; I do not want to see floats.
- A member owing $5.00 or more cannot check anything out (nonzero exit, name them in the stderr message) until they pay down: `pay NAME --amount X.XX` -- rejected if zero, negative, or more than they owe.
- `add-book ISBN --title T --copies N`: duplicate ISBN is a nonzero exit that mentions the ISBN and leaves the db byte-for-byte untouched; copies must be >= 1.
- A member can't have two copies of the same ISBN at once, and can't return what they don't have.
- `hold ISBN --member NAME`: FIFO waitlist. Refuse (nonzero) if copies are currently available (just check it out!), if they're already in the queue, or if they already have the book out. When a copy frees up, ONLY the head of the queue may check it out -- everyone else gets a nonzero exit until the head takes their copy (which removes them from the queue and the next person becomes head).
- `member NAME`: their current loans as `ISBN<TAB>due=YYYY-MM-DD` lines sorted by ISBN, then a final `fines=$X.XX` line. Someone we've never seen just prints `fines=$0.00`, exit 0.
- Unknown ISBN anywhere: nonzero, stderr mentions the ISBN.

`python3 test_library.py` is the acceptance suite -- that's the contract, don't edit it.
